channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: God of War Laufey Could Release as Early as February 2027
item.content:encoded:
Jason Schreier has said in a new YouTube video that God of War: Laufey could release as early as February 2027.

YouTube star Jason Schreier (sorry Jason, I know you’ll hate me saying that) has once again taken to YouTube in another video to talk about the impact of GTA 6, and what its release is having on the industry. One of the games he mentioned was God of War Laufey, which, according to Schreier, could release as early as February 2027.

God of War Laufey Release Date

According to Schreier, he said that he “wouldn’t be surprised if that [God of War Laufey] gets announced for February or March pretty soon”.

So, two things there, it seems like PlayStation and Santa Monica are aiming for their next God of War blockbuster to hit early next year, but also that the announcement could be made “soon”.

As of the time of writing, PlayStation has yet to announce a new State of Play, but Gamescom Opening Night Live is only around 60 days away, and it’s not unheard of for PlayStation to give Keighley a big announcement or two. In December 2024, PlayStation and Naughty Dog let Keighley reveal Intergalactic: The Heretic Prophet at The Game Awards.

RELATED – God Of War Laufey Actress: ‘This Is Just The Beginning’

Whatever happens with the announcement, though, it does seem like if God of War Laufey is announced for a February 2027 release, that’s going to mean an even more stacked month.

While not the next Mr Beast (yet?!), Jason Schreier’s new YouTube channel has some interesting videos and conversations about the industry, so check out his YouTube video below about why GTA 6 is scaring off other games, and perhaps subscribe if you like his content.

What do you think of God of War Laufey potentially releasing in February 2027?  Leave your thoughts down in the comments and in the official Insider Gaming Discord.

In other news, read about what one EA executive said about the use of AI in game development. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Tom Henderson is Insider Gaming's Co-Founder and Editor-In-Chief. When he's not running one of the industry's leading independent video game websites, he's probably playing an FPS like Call of Duty,…

More from Tom Henderson

item.title: GameStop CEO Gives Up $35 Billion Payout To Focus On eBay Purchase
item.content:encoded:
GameStop’s CEO really wants to own eBay. The company and its CEO, Ryan Cohen, announced on Wednesday that it was withdrawing plans for a performance bonus payout for Cohen.

The payout would’ve been worth up to $35 billion had specific market cap and profit milestones been hit.

But now, Cohen is no longer worried about hitting those goals and getting that money. Instead, the company’s primary focus is now on purchasing the long-running auction platform.

GameStop Won’t Give Up Hope

In May, GameStop made an unexpected $55 billion offer to purchase eBay. The deal would’ve seen eBay acquired by way of 50% cash and 50% GameStop stock, with Cohen acting as dual CEO of both entities.

Just over a week after the proposal, however, eBay rejected the bid.

“We have concluded that your proposal is neither credible nor attractive,” eBay Chairman of the Board of Directors Paul S. Pressler said at the time.

  • Grand Theft Paywall | GTA 6 Locks Five Single-Player Stores Behind $100 Price-Tag

That didn’t stop Cohen and GameStop from pushing forward. In fact, the company is planning to release “additional materials regarding its proposed acquisition of eBay this week, including a detailed presentation of the strategic rationale and operational plan for the combined company.”

Cohen says that he wants GameStop leadership “fully focused on GameStop’s operating performance and its proposed eBay acquisition.”

The release makes it clear that this latest announcement isn’t an official offer or solicitation for the company. Rather, it’s for “informational purposes only”.

As of publication, eBay has a market cap of roughly $49 billion, with shares trading around $110.56. GameStop’s, on the other hand, are trading at $21.36 per share, with a total market cap of $9.58 billion, over five times lower than eBay.

What do you think of GameStop’s continuing efforts to buy eBay? Leave your thoughts down in the comments and in the official Insider Gaming Discord.

In other news, read about what one EA executive said about the use of AI in game development. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

item.title: Fortnite is Bringing Battle Pass Items to the Shop for the First Time
item.content:encoded:
Fortnite has just announced Unvaulted, in which beloved outfits from previous battle passes will be added to the shop.

This is the first time we’ve seen Fortnite make such a decision. Until now, there has been a clear distinction between outfits acquired through the battle pass and those available in the shop, with no overlap. However, this new decision will allow players to obtain outfits they may have missed in previous battle passes.

Fortnite Unvaulted Brings Battle Pass Outfits to the Shop on June 28

A new announcement for ‘Unvaulted,’ the official Fortnite account on X (formerly Twitter), reveals that battle pass items will be available in the shop starting on June 28. The first set of items will be released as bundles and will include Marvel outfits from Chapter 5, Season 4. Some of the outfits featured in the Unvaulted trailer include Gwenpool, War Machine, Peelverine, Captain Jones, and Mysterio. Be sure to check out the announcement trailer below:

More Insider Gaming: Fortnite Adding Five New Sprites & Soccer Stadium in Next Update

However, this is much more than just acquiring the Marvel outfits from Chapter 5 Season 4; Fortnite has opened the floodgates to something way bigger. Players can now have a chance to acquire some of the rarest skins ever seen in Fortnite, which were released in previous battle passes and players never got the opportunity to get their hands on them again. Some of the most popular Battle Pass skins include Tony Stark, Midas, Spider-Man, and more.

What are your thoughts on Fortnite Unvaulted bringing battle pass items to the shop? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about PlayStation cracking down on close to 1000 more shovelware games. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
